title: LF03 Skillset
subtitle: Integrating clients into computer networks
intro: LF03 links networking technology with operational practice around client integration.
The focus is on planning, implementation, testing, and secure operation of client network environments.

subsections.title: Classify networks in the training company
items: Describe network segments in the company
Explain the role of clients, servers, and infrastructure
Use basic networking terms correctly
Place network tasks in a team context
Name operational requirements for networks

subsections.title: Distinguish main components of computer networks
items: Differentiate client, server, switch, router, and access point
Classify network segments logically
Compare typical client types in the company
Assign server types and services at a basic level
Roughly classify data center environments

subsections.title: Understand how data transfer works
items: Describe data flow between two endpoints
Explain basic transmission paths in networks
Recognize transmission error sources
Classify latency and throughput at a basic level
Trace communication flow in simple network scenarios

subsections.title: Distinguish network media and access
items: Compare copper, fiber, and wireless appropriately
Differentiate access methods to network media at a basic level
Align media selection with intended use
Name disturbance risks per medium
Prepare a simple media check

subsections.title: Apply Ethernet and TCP/IP
items: Classify Ethernet as the LAN standard in operations
Apply TCP/IP basics during troubleshooting
Assign default gateway, DNS, and DHCP correctly
Check basic network parameters for clients
Trace basic IP communication

subsections.title: Assess network topologies and structured cabling
items: Differentiate star, tree, and other topologies
Name simple pros and cons of topologies
Explain structured cabling at a basic level
Assign patch panels, outlets, and cable routing
Justify cabling decisions in a practical way

subsections.title: Classify layer models and addressing
items: Differentiate OSI and TCP/IP thinking in daily work
Correctly distinguish MAC and IP addresses
Apply subnet masks at a basic level
Detect addressing errors in client setups
Classify network address, broadcast, and host portion

subsections.title: Select network components and standards
items: Select suitable network cards for a client
Compare basic functions of switches and routers
Name relevant network standards for the use case
Estimate compatibility between components
Prepare procurement decisions technically

subsections.title: Plan and implement client integration
items: Capture client requirements systematically
Integrate a client into an existing network structure
Classify cloud services in the work context
Provide user login and basic access
Execute integration steps with documentation

subsections.title: Test and maintain network function
items: Perform targeted connectivity tests
Check name resolution in a structured way
Apply quick tests for initial diagnosis
Prepare maintenance measures
Create test logs for support cases

subsections.title: Configure network components
items: Set basic parameters for network cards
Prepare basic switch and router configurations
Configure network devices to fit operations
Follow secure standard configurations
Document configuration changes traceably

subsections.title: Implement data and network security
items: Classify RAID and backup basics for operations
Compare simple backup strategies
Apply basic security measures for client networks
Recognize security risks in integration early
Document security-related residual points

subsections.title: Calculate availability and downtime
items: Translate availability values into downtime
Relate service goals to daily operations
Prepare downtime for decision-making
Assess the effect of redundancy at a basic level
Communicate calculation results to the audience

subsections.title: Power supply and green IT in network operations
items: Consider power supply basics for network components
Roughly estimate energy demand in network operations
Name green IT measures for network environments
Argue for energy-efficient operating modes
Include sustainability aspects in decisions
